response.justification: Candidate has strong full-stack and geospatial hobby experience (GISCollective, Green Map) but lacks the core technical requirement: modern C++ development at a commercial level. The only C/C++ experience is a 6-month stint at National Instruments over 10 years ago, and there is no Rust experience as an alternative. No demonstrated background in routing pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or large-scale distributed-systems engineering in compiled systems languages. Primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which do not transfer directly to the role's stack.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective and Open Green Map projects
Long track record as a senior IC with testing/TDD discipline and CI/CD experience
Some low-latency systems exposure via DLang work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
concerns: No recent or substantial modern C++ experience; no Rust experience
No navigation/routing pipeline or production map-tiling background
Limited evidence of distributed-systems work (fault tolerance, consistency, service discovery) at required scale

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain exposure via GIS projects
